claim.claim: The Zorbon is a subject of conspiratorial belief, with claims that it is connected to mushrooms and radio signals, and that tinfoil hats provide protection from it.
claim.speaker: Johnny

candidatePages.rationale: The page confirms the Zorbon has fungal qualities (consistent with 'mushrooms'), that tinfoil repels its influence (consistent with 'tinfoil hats provide protection'), and that it controls minds (consistent with conspiratorial belief), though 'radio signals' is not mentioned on the page, the overall claim is largely covered.
candidatePages.excerpt: The Zorbon is a supposed entity that controls the minds of the unsuspecting. His machinations can be repelled by tinfoil, and so those that try to avoid his influence fashion clothing, like hats, out of foil. He's been described has having fungal qualities.

claim.claim: A Fane is a structure whose existence and size are sustained by a magical object called a heart.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The page describes a fane as the heart's home/dwelling place, not as a structure sustained by a magical object called a heart; rather, the heart lives *in* the fane and controls it, and the heart itself is the entity (not a magical object).
candidatePages.excerpt: hearts live in a place called a *fane*. its lik their house but its smaller on the outside and they have lots of control in it.

claim.claim: The size of a Fane is directly related to the amount of magical energy available to the heart sustaining it.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The page explicitly states that the size of a fane is based on how much magic the heart has, and that giving more magic to a heart makes it bigger.
candidatePages.excerpt: the size is based on how much magic they have and you can give more magic to a heart to make it bigger!!

claim.claim: Multiple hearts can jointly maintain a single Fane, a practice used by large organizational headquarters to sustain large footprints.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The page describes multiple small fanes merging into one big fane so hearts can coexist, not multiple hearts jointly maintaining a single fane from the outset; the mechanism is merging of separate fanes, not joint maintenance.
candidatePages.excerpt: sometimes a bunch of small fanes merge into a big 1 and then all the hearts can hang out!

claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The page mentions dockworkers and warehouse employees but does not specifically reference factories, scrapyards, or trucks carting goods.
candidatePages.excerpt: Sableclutch is dominated by the dockworkers and warehouse employees that ply their trade on the river.

claim.claim: The area is served by a trolley station.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The page does not mention any trolley station serving Sableclutch.

claim.claim: The local transit system uses trams (also called trolleys) that materialize at stations on a scheduled basis.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: Goblinoid creatures are commonly employed as workers in the factories of the area, and can be seen commuting in workman's attire.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The page does not mention goblinoid creatures or their employment in the factories of Sableclutch.

claim.claim: Skinwalkers are difficult to identify by sight alone, requiring a significantly higher DC than identifying whether someone is mute through conversation.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: The Masque District is an area of the city that contains hotels and serves as a tourist destination.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The wiki page contains no information about the Masque District or its role as a hotel/tourist area.

claim.claim: The tram system uses numbered stations, with at least one station designated 'Void 2' and a destination station designated 'Prime' or 'Zero'.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: The tram line is operated by Prime Meridian.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: The page states that Prime Meridian originated as a transportation company operating horse-pulled trams and still operates trains, supporting the claim that tram operations are associated with PM.
candidatePages.excerpt: PM started as a small transportation company back when a horse-pulled tram was the _height_ of public transport.

claim.claim: The tram stop at void one serves as a central location on its line, with many passengers disembarking there.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: The residential area the party is heading toward is wealthy enough that all its inhabitants can afford private cars, making public transport unavailable into that part of the city.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: A taxi fare into the wealthy residential district costs approximately three silver pieces.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: Two entities known as Smith and Mute are capable of disguising themselves as ordinary office workers to blend into urban environments.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: One of the two entities (referred to as Mute) communicates only through a gargling sound rather than speech, suggesting an inability to speak normally.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: Smith and Mute are shapeshifting creatures that can grow approximately a foot and a half in size when dropping their disguise.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: In their true form, Smith and Mute appear as large, pink, fleshy, naked mole rat-like creatures whose clothing melts into their bodies.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: In their true form, Smith and Mute have faces where the eyes sink into featureless sockets and most other facial features dissolve back into the skin.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: The flesh of the mound creatures is stringy in texture, allowing a hand to be pushed into it, and items can be found embedded within the flesh.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster

candidatePages.rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

claim.claim: Black line badges can be found concealed inside the flesh of the mound creatures, having been absorbed into their bodies.
claim.speaker: Gamemaster
